_doc: §19 regulatory deflection job aid · productionised. Each rep response is quoted verbatim from brief §19. The rep reads the verbatim line out loud — does not paraphrase, does not improvise. The escalation_criteria field defines when this row is insufficient and compliance escalation is the correct move. Rossett & Schafer (2007): point-of-use support; learning objective is performance WITH the aid, not memorisation.

rows.what_it_means: Know Your Customer · identity verification at signup
rows.rep_response_verbatim: We run KYC at signup. Takes 10 minutes for the owner. We've done it for 12,000+ businesses; the process is smoother than your bank's.
rows.escalation_criteria: Escalate if the buyer asks about specific document acceptance lists, beneficial-ownership thresholds, country-specific KYC variants, or PEP (Politically Exposed Person) handling beyond a 'yes we check' answer.

rows.rep_response_verbatim: Every transaction is monitored. You will not see this as a customer unless something gets flagged, which is rare and we walk you through it.
rows.escalation_criteria: Escalate if the buyer asks for transaction-monitoring rule details, SAR (Suspicious Activity Report) filing volumes, false-positive rates, or how flags are surfaced into their accounting workflow.

rows.rep_response_verbatim: Every card transaction over €30 triggers SCA. Your team will see a push to approve. Takes 2 seconds.
rows.escalation_criteria: Escalate if the buyer asks about SCA delegation, exemption-engine logic (low-value, trusted-beneficiary, transaction-risk-analysis exemptions), or merchant-side SCA flows on a specific corridor.

rows.what_it_means: EU Payment Services Directive · open banking framework
rows.rep_response_verbatim: We're PSD2-compliant. The short version: it's the regulation that lets us be additive to your bank, not a replacement.
rows.escalation_criteria: Escalate on ANY PSD2 follow-up question — open-banking consent flows, third-party-provider (TPP) registration, AISP/PISP licensing, RTS on SCA, cross-border passporting. PSD2 is reference-only on the sales side; depth lives with compliance.

rows.what_it_means: EU data protection · where your data lives + what we do with it
rows.rep_response_verbatim: Your card data lives in the EU. We don't sell it. We don't share it. Here's our DPA — happy to send it over.
rows.escalation_criteria: Escalate if the buyer asks about specific cross-border transfer mechanisms (SCCs, adequacy decisions for non-EU regions e.g. Switzerland, UK, US Data Privacy Framework), sub-processor lists, DSR (data subject request) timelines for ex-employee histories, or audit-clause language in the DPA.

honest_carve_out.principle: If the buyer goes deeper than these 5 rows: defer to compliance. The carve-out is not a weakness; it's the trust move.
